t_ch: 可可利亚大人…感谢你把我抚养成人，感谢你把选择的权利留给了我。
t_en: Madam Cocolia... I am grateful to you for raising me, and for allowing me the privilege of choice.

t_ch: 可是…对不起，母亲。这最后一次，我不能站在你身边。
t_en: But... I am sorry, mother. On this, our final occasion, I cannot stand with you.

t_ch: 你说人性的深处是愚蠢和畏缩，也许不假，绝境会映出人心中最阴暗的一面。
t_en: You say that conceit and cowardice lie in the depths of human nature — perhaps you're right... desperation sheds light on the darkest recesses of the heart.

t_ch: …但你却错过了那些在绝境中努力生存、努力奋战的人。我看到了他们身上的光芒，在禁区前线、在下层区、在被你忽略的角落里。
t_en: ...But what about those simply struggling for survival, fighting for something better? I've witnessed their light on the front lines, in the Underworld... in places you have overlooked.

t_ch: 先祖用双手建起了这座城市，在风雪摇曳中奋力延续文明。就算这个世界注定分崩离析，通向结局的路途也该由人类亲手铺就——
t_en: Our ancestors built this city — striving in the bitter snow to prolong our civilization. Even if this world is doomed to fall apart, we should allow humanity to pave the way to its final outcome...

t_ch: ——而不是把命运交给这枚祸种！
t_en: ...Not hand over our fate to this seed of ruin!

t_ch: 我们是由普通人选出的守护者，母亲！我们的职责是「存护」由人类建立的世界！我们不是神明，不是裁判！
t_en: We are guardians, mother! Chosen from among the masses! Our duty is to preserve the world built by humanity! We are not gods! We are not arbiters!

t_ch: 你想要把人性踩在脚下，同时扮演裁判和神明——这种事，我不能容许！
t_en: You seek to crush human nature underfoot while masquerading as an... as an arbiter and a god! I cannot let you!

t_ch: 这样啊…你的选择是…这样啊。我了解了，布洛妮娅。
t_en: So... this is your choice. I understand, Bronya.

t_ch: 可惜…可惜你看不到美好的世界了。你无法突破思想的桎梏…知道吗？你本该是…新世界的「母亲」。
t_en: Pity... Such a pity that you will never see that resplendent world... Unable to free yourself from the shackles of your mind... You know what? You were supposed to be... the mother of the new world.

t_ch: 地面…地面在震动！怎么回事？！
t_en: The ground... it's shaking! What's happening!?

t_ch: 不妙……
t_en: Bad sign...

t_ch: 这座城市…贝洛伯格的命运已经注定，它的未来将在我们手中展开。
t_en: The fate of this city... of Belobog... is sealed. Its future will unfold in our hands.

t_ch: 而你们…将成为这个新世界的基石！
t_en: And you... will become the foundation of the new world!

t_ch: 被冰封的虚卒遗骸遍布这条山道，看来就连残暴的军团也无法抵御寒潮的威力。
t_en: The frozen remains of Voidrangers are scattered along the mountain path. Even the ruthless Antimatter Legion is helpless against the might of the Eternal Freeze.

t_ch: 但那虚卒的遗体竟保存得如此完整，你不禁开始思考…也许三月七的怀疑有些道理。
t_en: The Voidranger remains are preserved remarkably well in the ice. You can't help but wonder... perhaps March 7th is right.

t_ch: ——无论如何，绝对不能给这些东西解冻。
t_en: —Either way, you think that it's best not to unfreeze them.

t_ch: 比、比史瓦罗还大一万倍的机器人！
t_en: Th—This robot's a thousand times bigger than Svarog!

t_ch: …贝洛伯格的未来，在我手中！
t_en: ...The future of Belobog rests in my hands!

t_ch: 迎接你们的末日吧！
t_en: Behold, the end is upon you!

t_ch: 引擎…粉碎他们！
t_en: Engine... break them!

t_ch: 铲平山峰！
t_en: Raze to oblivion!

t_ch: …必须阻止它的行动！
t_en: ...We have to stop it!

t_ch: 这样的巨物，我们该怎么摧毁它？
t_en: How can we bring a giant like this down?

t_ch: 听啊…这力量在涌动…在歌唱……
t_en: Listen to the power surge... sing...

t_ch: 星核给予我的承诺，才是这个世界唯一的希望……
t_en: The Stellaron's promise is the only hope for this world...

t_ch: 七百年前，它驱逐了反物质军团——今天，它也会将你们的存在一并抹消！
t_en: Just as it dispelled the Antimatter Legion <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago, so too will it wipe you from the face of this world!

t_ch: …你已经失去最后一个离开这里的机会了。
t_en: ...You lost your final chance to leave this place.

t_ch: 不自量力…那就让你绝望吧！
t_en: Fools... You will know the taste of despair!

t_ch: 这不可能……
t_en: No... It can't be...

t_ch: 「存护」的意志已经作出了选择……
t_en: The will of Preservation has made its choice...

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的未来也不再由守护者一个人决定。
t_en: Hmph. The future of Belobog is no longer just the guardian's to decide.

t_ch: 你们对这个世界的困境一无所知…对于等待着它的结局一无所知！
t_en: You know nothing of the plight of this world... nothing of the wait for its final end!

t_ch: 我们清楚，我们非常清楚。
t_en: On the contrary, we know it well.

t_ch: 就算面对风雪，面对裂界…这座城市里的每一个人依旧没有放弃希望，依旧憧憬着明天。
t_en: Even in the face of the blizzard and the Fragmentum... the people of this city never gave up hope, never stopped yearning for tomorrow.

t_ch: 哪怕注定走入灭亡，我们也会手牵着手，勇敢地走入那片黑暗——
t_en: Even if we are doomed to destruction, we will go bravely, walking hand in hand into the darkness...

t_ch: 不，有我们在，那种结局绝对不会发生！
t_en: No! That end won't come to pass while we're around!

t_ch: 我绝不…允许！
t_en: I will not... let you!

t_ch: 我会解救所有人……
t_en: I... will save everyone...!

t_ch: 以贝洛伯格所有人的名义，我命令你起身——「造物引擎」！
t_en: In the name of every citizen of Belobog, I command you to rise... Engine of Creation!

t_ch: 不…服从我的命令！我的！
t_en: No... No! Obey my commands! Obey me!

t_ch: {RUBY_B#祂}星核{RUBY_E#}的力量与我同在…
t_en: The power of the Stellaron is with me...

t_ch: 你们…不过是旧世界崩溃前的垂死挣扎！
t_en: You are but cinders of the Old World soon to become ashes!

t_ch: 在列车组、布洛妮娅和希儿的共同努力下，可可利亚战败，{RUBY_B#祂}星核{RUBY_E#}制造的威胁被暂时解除。
t_en: With the combined efforts of the Crew, Bronya, and Seele, Cocolia was defeated and the threat created by the Stellaron was temporarily averted.

t_ch: 但贝洛伯格面临的危机还远未结束。在布洛妮娅的带领下，上下层区的人们将再次团结起来，继续为了生存而战……
t_en: But the crisis facing Belobog is far from over. Led by Bronya, the people of the Overworld and the Underworld will once again unite and continue to fight for their survival...

t_ch: 列车组在雅利洛-Ⅵ的探险暂时告一段落，开拓者们在星间的旅途才刚刚启程。
t_en: The Crew's adventure in Jarilo-VI has come to an end for the time being, but the Trailblazer's journey among the stars has just begun.

t_ch: 如果可可利亚拿布洛妮娅来要挟我们……
t_en: If Cocolia threatens us by taking Bronya hostage...

t_ch: 必须把她救下来。下层区…不，这个世界都靠她了。
t_en: We have to save her. The Underworld... no, the entire planet relies on her.

t_ch: …姬子，是姬子！
t_en: ...Himeko, it's Himeko!

t_ch: 喂喂！听得见吗？信号真差……
t_en: Hello? Can you hear me? This signal is terrible...

t_ch: 姬子！你可算想到我们啦！
t_en: Himeko! You finally spared a thought for us!

t_ch: 什么啊，我和瓦尔特先生可是时刻在轨道上挂念着各位呢，这次的开拓之旅很精彩喔。
t_en: What are you talking about — Welt and I have been up on the star rail missing you this whole time! You've got quite the Trailblazing expedition on your hands.

t_ch: 这大家伙到底是怎么动起来的……不过，现在还是让它乖乖别动比较好？接下来，就看你们的啦！
t_en: So how does this big guy work...? Or more importantly — how do you stop him from working? Over to you guys, I guess!

t_ch: 我们来掩护你！
t_en: We'll cover you!

t_ch: 这里是…宇宙？
t_en: Is this... the universe?

t_ch: 我不是正在和可可利亚战斗吗，为什么会在这里……
t_en: But I was fighting Cocolia. Why am I here...?

t_ch: …上次来到这里时，纳努克瞥了我一眼，难道……
t_en: ...The last time I came here, I drew Nanook's gaze. Could it be...

t_ch: 这把枪是……可可利亚的武器？
t_en: This spear... isn't that Cocolia's weapon?

t_ch: 结束了吗……
t_en: Is it over...?

t_ch: 母亲…不知道你最后有没有从梦里醒来……
t_en: Mother... Did you ever finally wake up from that dream...?

t_ch: 布洛妮娅……
t_en: Bronya...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，丹恒——我们成功了，对吧？摆脱了可可利亚和她手里的星核，雅利洛-Ⅵ的危机就算是解除了？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Dan Heng — is that mission complete? We got rid of Cocolia and the Stellaron, so the crisis on Jarilo-VI should be over now, right?

t_ch: 我不清楚，三月。或许得等我们回到列车上以后，听听姬子和瓦尔特先生怎么说。
t_en: I'm not sure, March. We should see what Himeko and Mr. Yang have to say when we get back to the Express.

t_ch: 我能明显感觉到，笼罩这座山岭的风雪正在逐渐消退。但是……
t_en: It certainly seems that the blizzard around this mountain is starting to gradually subside. However...

t_ch: …但是？别卖关子啦，丹恒！
t_en: ...However what? Don't leave us on a cliffhanger, Dan Heng!

t_ch: …也许可可利亚的话并非满篇胡言乱语。星核对这个世界造成的影响已经太深，恐怕已经到了无法逆转的地步。
t_en: ...Perhaps there was some truth among Cocolia's ravings. Over such a long period of time, the Stellaron has surely had a huge impact on this world. It's possible that the changes have become irreversible.

t_ch: 但我们早就夸下海口…
t_en: But we said we could reverse it...

t_ch: 还是要让姬子来下定论。
t_en: We should wait to see what Himeko says.

t_ch: 对呀，姬子姐姐才是这方面的专家，对吧？
t_en: Yeah. Himeko is an expert on this kind of thing, right?

t_ch: 贝洛伯格——
t_en: Belobog...

t_ch: 你们在说什么？是你们最先承诺能帮忙解决「星核」的问题，我们才——
t_en: What!? You promised from the very start that you could reverse the effects of the Stellaron. Otherwise, why would we—

t_ch: 希儿！别激动。
t_en: Seele! Calm down.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}已经为我们的世界拼上了性命，你也看到了吧？如果贝洛伯格的命运真的如母亲所说，我相信他们也和你我一样心痛。
t_en: {NICKNAME} has risked their life for our world. You saw it yourself. If Belobog's fate really is as Mother predicts, I am sure they will be just as saddened as you or I.

t_ch: …抱歉，我太激动了。我只是很难接受，难道这一切都是白费力气……
t_en: ...Sorry, I got a bit worked up. It just upsets me to think that all of this was for nothing...

t_ch: 当然不是白费力气，希儿。抬头看看吧，这是我有生以来第一次看到天空——是真实的天空，不是被「存护」的力量创造出来的宽慰。
t_en: Of course it wasn't for nothing, Seele. Look up — have you ever been able to see the sky before in your life? I mean, the real sky, not the comforting fabrication created by the power of Preservation.

t_ch: 我们从「星核」手中夺回了原生的天空…就算这片土地仍然在走向衰亡，至少我们也争取到了一些时间。这个结果已经足够好了，希儿。
t_en: We've taken back the sky that was stolen from us by the Stellaron long ago... Even if the earth below our feet is still dying, we at least bought ourselves some more time. That's better than nothing, Seele.

t_ch: …嗯。只要还有时间，我们就一定能找到修复世界的办法。
t_en: ...Okay. As long as we still have time, then I know we'll find a way to save the planet.

t_ch: 没错。但在那之前，我们还有太多工作要做……
t_en: Right. But before that, we have lots of work to do...

t_ch: 谢谢你们，三月，丹恒，还有{NICKNAME}。我知道这颗星球只是你们旅途中的小小一站，但你们做出的贡献一定会被贝洛伯格人永远铭记。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}, March, and Dan Heng. I know this planet is just one small stop on your long journey, but your help will be forever remembered by the people of Belobog.

t_ch: 我向你们承诺，一定会带领这座城市走向复兴，绝对不抛下上下层区的任何一人…绝对不辜负我们这一番奋战。
t_en: I promise you, we will lead this city and its people back to prosperity, both the Overworld and the Underworld... This hard fight will not have been for nothing.

t_ch: 但愿有朝一日我们还能再见…{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I hope one day we shall meet again, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 人类…总是软弱愚蠢，总是不自量力。
t_en: *vicious laughter* Humanity... so weak and foolish. Always overestimating their abilities.

t_ch: ——我来赐予你绝望吧！
t_en: Let me bestow upon you... despair!

t_ch: 结束了……
t_en: It's over...

t_ch: 母亲…您有没有从梦里…醒来呢？
t_en: Mother... Did you wake up from that dream...?

t_ch: 哇，这一次的开拓…真不容易。丹恒、{NICKNAME}——我们成功啦！呃，不过「星核」该怎么办？以前都是杨叔处理的……
t_en: Wow... that was a tough trailblazing expedition. Dan Heng, {NICKNAME} — we made it! But, uh, what are we gonna do with the Stellaron? It's usually Mr. Yang who handles it...

t_ch: 我已经通知了姬子和瓦尔特先生，这颗「星核」就交给他们来封印。
t_en: I've informed Himeko and Mr. Yang already. They'll seal the Stellaron.

t_ch: 好耶！这下，这个世界的危机就算是解除了吧？
t_en: Nice! The crisis on this world is basically over, right?

t_ch: …风雪凌厉，难以骤停。
t_en: ...The storm still rages on.

t_ch: 「星核」封印以后，寒潮会慢慢消退。这个世界的裂界不会再凶猛扩张，但也不会凭空消失。雅利洛-Ⅵ得到了重获生机的可能性，但那需要时间……
t_en: Now that the Stellaron is sealed, the Eternal Freeze will slowly recede. The Fragmentum won't aggressively expand anymore, but nor will it disappear. Jarilo-VI has a chance to return to life, but it will take time...

t_ch: 这些，就要看他们自己的努力了。
t_en: It will depend on the efforts of the people here.

t_ch: 各位…谢谢你们。
t_en: Thank you... all of you.

t_ch: 别勉强自己。
t_en: Don't be too hard on yourself.

t_ch: 谢谢，{NICKNAME}…我没事，不用担心。
t_en: Thank you {NICKNAME}... I—I'm fine. Don't worry.

t_ch: 接下来有什么打算？
t_en: What do you want to do now?

t_ch: 对于贝洛伯格来说…这场胜利只是个开始。
t_en: This victory is only the beginning for Belobog.

t_ch: 现在我也开不出玩笑了…
t_en: Can't think of anything funny to say...

t_ch: 总要有人保持乐观啊。况且…我们已经迈过了最艰难的一关。
t_en: Hehe, start thinking, we're going to need all the humor we can get... At least the biggest hurdle is behind us.

t_ch: 要做的事还有很多…那台「造物引擎」引发的巨大动静，城里的居民一定都察觉到了。许多疑惑需要解释，许多真相需要公开……
t_en: There is still much to do... The people will have felt the effects of the Engine of Creation. There are so many questions to answer, so many truths to reveal...

t_ch: 我只是不知道要怎么让所有人接受这一切……
t_en: I don't know whether I can get the people to accept all this...

t_ch: 把真相告诉所有人吧。
t_en: Tell everyone the truth.

t_ch: 真的可以吗……
t_en: Can I really do that...?

t_ch: 让民众知道母亲的真实目的…告诉他们这个维系了七百年的谎言……
t_en: Tell everyone about Mother's true motives... about a lie perpetrated for <unbreak>700</unbreak> years...

t_ch: 不——绝对不行。
t_en: No... No, no way.

t_ch: 把真相伪装起来吧…
t_en: Cover up the truth...

t_ch: 你的意思是……
t_en: You mean...

t_ch: 用一个谎言去遮蔽另一个——但如果这样做，我……
t_en: ...Use a lie to conceal a lie. But if I do that, then...

t_ch: 就这么做吧，布洛妮娅。
t_en: You have to, Bronya.

t_ch: …希儿？
t_en: ...Seele?

t_ch: 我…我不知道该怎么解释，我只是觉得没有别的办法。
t_en: I... I don't know how to explain it... but I don't think there's any other way.

t_ch: 在下层，我们每天都在对小孩子重复同一句话：「明天会好起来的」——谁都知道那是在骗人，但至少能让他们带着希望入睡。
t_en: In the Underworld, we tell the kids "things will be better tomorrow." Everyone knows it's a lie, but it gets them to sleep with some hope.

t_ch: 如果把在这里发生的事说出去…你能想象后果吗？人们会失望的，如果连守护者都无法相信——换做是我，我会不知道自己还能相信什么…还能相信谁。
t_en: Can you imagine the consequences if we told the people what happened here? They'd be devastated. If we can't trust the guardians, who — or what — can we trust...?

t_ch: …母亲是为了「存护」贝洛伯格而牺牲的。
t_en: ...Mother died to preserve Belobog.
...What?

t_ch: 从天外降临的旅行者口中，她知晓了「星核」的秘密。她也知道，第一任大守护者阿丽萨•兰德没能摧毁「星核」……
t_en: The visitors from beyond the sky told her the secret of the Stellaron. She knew that Alisa Rand, the first supreme guardian, had failed to destroy it...

t_ch: …但她还是决定挑战这股远超人类想象的力量。大守护者可可利亚牺牲自己，驱散了笼罩贝洛伯格的阴霾。
t_en: ...and yet she decided to challenge its power — a power beyond human comprehension. Supreme Guardian Cocolia sacrificed herself to dispel the dark clouds enveloping this city.

t_ch: ——从现在起，这就是「真相」…你们认为呢？
t_en: ...From here on out, that's the "truth" the world will hear. What do you think?

t_ch: 起码它有点「存护」的希望。
t_en: It keeps the hope of preservation alive, at least.

t_ch: 合乎情理…至少极难证伪。
t_en: It's logical... and hard to disprove.

t_ch: 为什么非得说谎不可呢…不知道啦，我弃权，我还是更喜欢说真话。虽然…我也理解你们就是了。
t_en: Why do we have to lie? ...I'm abstaining — I like telling the truth. But... I get what you're trying to do.

t_ch: 我赞同，就这么说吧。
t_en: Agreed — let's use this version.

t_ch: 谢谢你的理解，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you for understanding, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 谎言永远是谎言…
t_en: A lie will always be a lie...

t_ch: 我明白。但…我必须保护相信筑城者的人民…
t_en: I understand, but... I must protect those that believe in the Architects...

t_ch: 把我们的功劳给抢了啊…
t_en: ...Maybe we include some of the cool stuff we did?

t_ch: 抱歉…我一定会想办法补偿你们。
t_en: Sorry... I'll be sure to make it up to you.

t_ch: 在很多人眼里，母亲是一位优秀的守护者…如果要保存真相，就把那些错误、疯狂、邪恶与梦想保存在我的记忆中…让我来背负就好。
t_en: For many people, mother was an outstanding guardian... The whole truth will live on in my mind — all the mistakes, madness, evil, and dreams therein... I will be the one to carry that burden.

t_ch: 既然注定要接过「大守护者」的职责…那我就必须舍弃天真的幻想。
t_en: Now I must take over the duties of the supreme guardian... and discard my naive fantasies.

t_ch: 抱歉，希儿。这个秘密，还请你和我一同把守…直到我们生命的尽头。
t_en: I'm sorry, Seele. Please protect this secret with me... till the end of our lives.

t_ch: 当然——我知道该怎么做。
t_en: Say no more — I know what to do.

t_ch: 谢谢你。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 那么，按照我们之前的约定…着手重建上下层之间的桥梁吧。
t_en: Well then, as promised, let's start rebuilding the bridge between the Overworld and the Underworld.

t_ch: 希儿，你能将「星核」被封印的消息带给下层区的大家吗？告诉他们，封锁很快就会结束…很快就能自由地呼吸了。
t_en: Seele, can you tell everyone in the Underworld that the Stellaron has been sealed? Tell them the blockade will soon be over... and they'll breathe freely again soon.

t_ch: 当然，交给我吧——我猜娜塔都等急了。
t_en: Of course, leave it to me — Nat's probably getting anxious about the delay.

t_ch: 有你帮忙传话，我就能放心赶回行政区了。必须通知杰帕德和希…呃……
t_en: While you do that, I'll head back to the Administrative District. I need to tell Gepard... Serv— ah...

t_ch: …你没事吧？！
t_en: ...Are you okay!?

t_ch: 怎么了，布洛妮娅？哪里不舒服吗？
t_en: What's wrong, Bronya? Do you feel sick?

t_ch: 我…没事。必须…快点动身……..
t_en: I'm... fine. I need to... hurry...

t_ch: 她的状况好像不太妙啊！怎么办怎么办…{NICKNAME}，想个法子呗？
t_en: She's exhausted! Quick, we need to think of something... {NICKNAME}, help me out here!

t_ch: 希儿，你带布洛妮娅回去吧。
t_en: Seele, take Bronya back with you.

t_ch: 去下层传话的任务就交给我们。
t_en: We'll go to the Underworld and pass on the message.

t_ch: …好。差点忘了，你们还在被城里的铁卫通缉…虽然我是下层区人，但起码不像你们这么显眼。
t_en: Right... I almost forgot, you're still wanted by the guards... I might be an Underworlder, but at least I don't stand out as much as you guys.

t_ch: 那就这么办吧，娜塔那边就交给你们了。为了布洛…不对，是为了整个贝洛伯格——麻烦你们，保守好这个秘密。
t_en: That's settled then. I'll leave Nat to you. Remember to keep the secret — for Bron— No, for Belobog's sake.

t_ch: 你还好吗？哪里不舒服…头昏？乏力？还是……
t_en: Are you alright? Are you hurt anywhere? Do you feel dizzy? Tired? Or...

t_ch: …我说不上来，希儿。全身上下…都感觉…很冰冷。
t_en: ...I don't know, Seele. I feel... cold... all over.

t_ch: 嗐，那不是很正常吗？这地方本来就冷得离谱…嘁……
t_en: Isn't that normal? This place is freezing... *sneeze* Ugh...

t_ch: 还是快离开这儿吧，不然连你也要冻出毛病了。
t_en: Let's get out of here before you catch a cold too.

t_ch: 别怕，我身子骨结实得很。来，扶好我…咱们走吧。
t_en: Don't worry, I'm a tough cookie. Here, hold on to me... let's go.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，刚才你突然出现在战场上，手里还提着那么酷拽的武器…到底发生了什么，也该告诉我们了吧？
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, wanna tell us what happened back there? Don't think you can just reappear on the battlefield with a cool weapon in your hands and skip the story.

t_ch: 我见到了「存护」克里珀…
t_en: I saw Qlipoth the Preservation...

t_ch: 唔…你在这个时候被琥珀王瞥了一眼？这也太巧了吧……
t_en: Uh... you drew the Amber Lord's gaze? Right at that moment? That's too much of a coincidence...

t_ch: 这是我第二次为你牺牲，三月…
t_en: That's the second time I've sacrificed myself for you, March...

t_ch: 呃…谢谢？但是这所答非所问呀……
t_en: Um... thank you? That's not answering my question though...

t_ch: 说时迟那时快，力量从天而降…
t_en: All of a sudden, I was infused with a great and terrible power...!

t_ch: 能不能正经点！咱是真的很好奇……
t_en: Be serious! I really wanna know...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}本人也不清楚吧？这不是第一次了。在空间站面对末日兽的时候，也发生过类似的事——还记得纳努克的瞥视吗。
t_en: {NICKNAME} probably doesn't know what happened either. This wouldn't be the first time... Something similar happened when we faced the Doomsday Beast on the space station. Remember that glance from Nanook?

t_ch: 我们原以为是因星核与「毁灭」有关，但现在看来并不简单——恐怕得与瓦尔特先生谈谈才能理出头绪。
t_en: Originally, we thought it was because the Stellaron was linked to the Destruction, but now it seems to be more than that. We might need to speak with Mr. Yang to understand what's going on.

t_ch: 先着眼于当下的事务吧，姬子和瓦尔特先生处理星核时，不方便打扰他们。返回列车再说。
t_en: Let's focus on the business at hand first. We shouldn't disrupt Himeko and Mr. Yang while they're dealing with the Stellaron. Let's talk once we're back on the Express.

t_ch: 嗯，也对，毕竟做事就得善始善终嘛…我们赶紧去找娜塔莎。
t_en: Mm, you're right, we need to finish what we started... Let's hurry up and find Natasha.

t_ch: 聆听贝洛伯格的声音。
t_en: Listen to the sound of Belobog

t_ch: 这座城市在哭喊…「存护」的力量在消褪。
t_en: The city is drowning in tears... The power of the Preservation is fading.

t_ch: 最后的最后…我们还是无法抗衡「星核」的意志。
t_en: We could not defy the will of the Stellaron after all...

t_ch: 可可利亚放弃了「存护」。
t_en: Cocolia abandoned Preservation.

t_ch: 没错…但我们无权责难。
t_en: Yes... But we have no right to judge.

t_ch: 这不是「存护」的真谛…
t_en: This is not what Preservation would have wanted...

t_ch: 你因何如此笃定？
t_en: Ah, a believer, I see?

t_ch: 过去的七百年，裂界每分每秒都在持续扩张。我们留给接替者的…没有信念或财富，唯有漫无边际的绝望。
t_en: The Fragmentum had been expanding for the past <unbreak>700</unbreak> years. There is no faith nor wealth left for our successors... only despair.

t_ch: 目睹誓言「存护」的家园在眼前逐渐消逝…是种折磨。再加上那挥之不去、扇惑人心的低语……
t_en: To see the home we vowed to preserve wither away... That in itself is torture. Not to mention the haunting whisper of temptation...

t_ch: 再坚定的意志也难免动摇，这一天注定会到来。祂的佑护…注定会离我们而去。
t_en: The day will come when even the strongest of wills cannot help but give in to such influence. It is only a matter of time before the Amber Lord forsakes us.

t_ch: ——总有不会动摇的人。
t_en: Some people can't be swayed.

t_ch: 那就让别人来「存护」吧。
t_en: Then let others carry on the will of Preservation.
You...

t_ch: 一介过客，却执意要肩负起一整个世界的命运……
t_en: You are but a stranger to us. And yet you seek to bond your destiny to ours...

t_ch: 也好。还有什么可失去…还有什么不能尝试呢？
t_en: So be it. What is there to lose when so much has already been lost...

t_ch: 去触碰琥珀的光芒吧，开拓者。看看你内心「存护」的意志是否足够强大…强大到能吸引祂的目光。
t_en: Touch the light of the Amber, trailblazer. See if your will can attract THEIR gaze.

t_ch: （就在这里等待朗道姐弟吧……）
t_en: (I'll wait for them here...)

t_ch: 呼…这地方…可真冷。
t_en: Brr... it's cold here...

t_ch: 这个大家伙…就是星核拘束装置吗？前人居然掌握了如此复杂的科技，真是壮观……
t_en: Is this... the Stellaron Imprisonment Device? They had such advanced technology back in the day.... impressive.

t_ch: 可可利亚大人…就是在此处战死的。
t_en: ...This is where Madam Cocolia died.

t_ch: …呵。死得其所。
t_en: ...Mm. She gave something back, in death.

t_ch: 该说说你想做什么了吧？
t_en: Is there anything you want to do here?

t_ch: 我还以为你是来吊唁的。
t_en: Did you come here to pay tribute?

t_ch: …大概吧。不过…我来这里的主要目的还是为了自己。
t_en: I think I'm mostly here for myself. Still...

t_ch: 我来这里，是为了完成一场「告别演出」——
t_en: I wanted to come here to perform for Cocolia. To say goodbye...

t_ch: 这历史性的一幕，还想请你们两个共同见证。
t_en: ...I'm glad you guys are here to witness it.

t_ch: 场地不算好，设备也有些简陋…见谅啦。
t_en: Not the nicest spot for a show, and the equipment could be better... sorry about that.

t_ch: 该上台了，姐姐。
t_en: The stage is yours, sister.

t_ch: 看着我，可可利亚！千万别把目光移开……
t_en: Look at me, Cocolia! Don't you dare look away...

t_ch: 因为我啊——
t_en: Because I...

t_ch: 再也！
t_en: will no longer...

t_ch: 不会！
t_en: live...

t_ch: 活在！
t_en: in...

t_ch: 你的！
t_en: in...

t_ch: 阴影中！！！
t_en: shadow!!!

t_ch: …痛快了？
t_en: ...happy?

t_ch: 哈，痛快。唯一的遗憾是看不到那家伙的表情……
t_en: ...Happy. I just wish I could've seen the look on her face.

t_ch: 太可惜了。
t_en: Shame.

t_ch: 好啦，该办的都办完了…{NICKNAME}，老弟——辛苦你们俩陪我折腾这么久。
t_en: Well, that's all from me... {NICKNAME}, Gepard, thanks for sticking by my side with me throughout all of this.

t_ch: 今晚…咱们就去老歌德那儿开个庆功宴吧！
t_en: As for tonight... let's go to Old Goethe's place and have a big feast to celebrate!

t_ch: …那必须你买单。
t_en: So... you're getting the tab?

t_ch: （这家伙，和之前遇到过的裂界生物有些不一样…）
t_en: (This thing seems a little different from the other Fragmentum creatures...)

t_ch: （她似乎没有主动进攻的欲望。也许可以避免一场恶战？）
t_en: (She doesn't seem to have any desire whatsoever to attack. Perhaps I can avoid a tough fight?)

t_ch: …你是谁？
t_en: ...Who are you?

t_ch: …你是在叫我吗？
t_en: ...Are you talking to me?

t_ch: 你…真实存在吗…？
t_en: You... are you real?

t_ch: 还是免不了要战斗吗？
t_en: Do I still have to fight?

t_ch: 我…不想…伤害别人……
t_en: I don't... want to hurt... anyone...

t_ch: 我命休矣…
t_en: I'm a goner...

t_ch: 我…没有恶意……
t_en: I... don't mean... trouble...

t_ch: 我已经很久…很久没见到过陌生人了。
t_en: I haven't seen a... stranger in a long, long time.

t_ch: 斯捷潘先生走了…他们也走了。所有人都离开了。
t_en: Mr. Stefan is gone... they've all gone. Everyone.

t_ch: 信任的人…亲近的人…全都离我而去。这座城市…抛弃了我。
t_en: The people I trusted... the people I was close to... they've all left me. This city... has abandoned me.

t_ch: （斯捷潘？我记得这个名字。）
t_en: (Stefan? I remember that name.)

t_ch: （难道她是……）
t_en: (Could she be...)

t_ch: 是你吗，希莉儿？
t_en: Is that you, Cyrille?

t_ch: 陌生人…你认识我？
t_en: Do you know me... stranger?

t_ch: 你…是斯捷潘先生派你来见我的吗？
t_en: You... Did Mr. Stefan send you to meet me?

t_ch: （撒谎）嗯，他让我救你出去。
t_en: (lying) Yes! He told me to come and save you.

t_ch: 不…不对。你身上…没有他的气息。
t_en: N—No. You don't have... his scent.

t_ch: 抱歉，我不认识这个人。
t_en: Sorry, I don't know him.

t_ch: 这样啊…没关系。我也感受到了…你身上的确没有他的气息。
t_en: Oh... that's okay. I can feel it too... You really don't have his scent on you at all.

t_ch: …没有人会到这里来找我了。我不够聪明，不够坚强，不够自立…但唯独这点我很清楚。我一直在欺骗自己，就这样过了太久…我厌倦了。
t_en: ...Nobody is coming here for me anymore. I'm not smart enough, not strong enough, not independent enough... but I know all that. I've been lying to myself all this time and living that way for so long... I'm so tired of it.

t_ch: 你…陌生人，我不明白…你怎么会知道我的名字？
t_en: You... stranger, I don't understand... How do you know my name?

t_ch: …罢了。我不想再深究…太多过问永远只会给我带来痛苦。始终如此……
t_en: ...Never mind. I don't want to get into it any further... too much questioning will only lead to more pain. It's always been the case...

t_ch: 你是我唯一的见证者…最后的见证者。这个事实已经足够让我欣慰…知道我不必在永恒的孤独中游荡……
t_en: You're my only witness... the final witness. That fact alone is comforting enough... knowing that I don't have to wander around in eternal loneliness...

t_ch: 我能为你做些什么？
t_en: What can I do for you?

t_ch: 你现在在想什么？
t_en: What are you thinking about right now?

t_ch: 我…有一事相求。
t_en: I... have a favor to ask.

t_ch: 我想知道…我的命运为何会是这般。我猜答案就被封存在这里，但我对这些谜语无能为力……
t_en: I want to know... why my fate is like this. I guess the answer is sealed in here, but there's nothing I can do about it...

t_ch: 你愿意帮我吗，陌生人？如果能找到答案…我愿意把有关自己的一切都告诉你。毫无保留。
t_en: Will you help me, stranger? If you can help me find the answer... I'll tell you everything there is about me. No secrets.

t_ch: 我的故事…也许并没有多么有趣。但或许你想听，对吗？希望你会想听……
t_en: My story... may not be that interesting, but perhaps you'd like to hear it, right? I hope you do...

t_ch: 我来帮你…我想听听你的故事。
t_en: I'll help you... I want to hear your story.

t_ch: 谢谢你。我…我害怕你会拒绝。
t_en: Thank you. I was afraid... that you'd say no.

t_ch: 如果我拒绝…
t_en: If I say no...

t_ch: …我害怕你会这么说。但是…我不能强迫你。我从来都没有想过要强迫谁。
t_en: ...I was afraid you'd say that, but... I can't force you. I would never force anyone to do anything.

t_ch: 也许我只有继续等待了。等待下一个不会再来的见证者经过……
t_en: Maybe I just have to keep waiting around. Wait for the next witness, who'll probably never come...

t_ch: 抱歉…我会帮你。
t_en: Sorry... I'll help you out.

t_ch: 我帮你，哪怕只是出于同情。
t_en: I'll help you, even if it's just out of sympathy.

t_ch: 真的吗？谢谢你……
t_en: Really? Thank you...

t_ch: 我无处可去，我就在这里等你。
t_en: I got no place to go. I'll be waiting for you here.

t_ch: 想放弃了也没关系…我不过是个影子，哪怕被欺骗也不会记恨…不会悲伤。
t_en: If you want to give up, that's okay... I'm just a shadow, and I'm not able to begrudge anyone, even if they trick me... And I don't feel sad either.

t_ch: 我…看清真相的面容了。
t_en: I... see the truth now.

t_ch: 谢谢你，陌生人……
t_en: Thank you, stranger...

t_ch: 你让我看清了真相，陌生人。我不知道该如何报答这份恩情…如你所见，现在的我什么都做不了。
t_en: You've shown me the truth, stranger. I don't know how to repay this kindness... as you can see, I can't exactly do anything right now.

t_ch: 但我至少还能履行之前的约定，把我的故事告诉你。你愿意驻足聆听吗？
t_en: But at least I can fulfill my previous promise to you, and tell you my story. Would you like to stop and listen?

t_ch: 我愿意。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: …你很温柔，陌生人。和斯捷潘先生一样温柔。
t_en: ...You are so gentle, stranger. As gentle as Mr. Stefan.

t_ch: 你不必勉强。
t_en: You don't have to.

t_ch: 不…这也是我最后的心愿。斯捷潘先生会答应的。
t_en: No... This is my final wish. Mr. Stefan will agree.

t_ch: ——我是希莉儿，贝洛伯格的大守护者。
t_en: — I'm Cyrille, Supreme Guardian of Belobog.

t_ch: 我自知不配登上如此高位。若不是姐姐当年无缘无故地离开…我根本不可能走上这条道路。
t_en: I know I don't deserve to be in such a high position. If it weren't for my sister going off then for no reason at all... I'd never have been able to take this path.

t_ch: 幸好还有斯捷潘先生在我身边——他是我的老师，挚友。他给了我无数宝贵的建议…帮我撑过了一个又一个危机。
t_en: Luckily, I have Mr. Stefan by my side — he is my teacher and dear friend. He's given me countless pieces of valuable advice... and helped me through crisis after crisis.

t_ch: 你为何在此徘徊？
t_en: Why do you linger around here?

t_ch: 因为疑惑…对我死因的疑惑。
t_en: I'm confused... confused about the cause of my death.

t_ch: 那些箱子谜题和你有什么关系？
t_en: What's your connection to the box puzzles?

t_ch: 它们…是我生命的终点。我之所以会在此徘徊…也许正是出于对自己死因的困惑。
t_en: They... are the end of my life. The reason I'm lingering around here... may be connected with my confusion about the cause of my death.

t_ch: 但你帮我解除了所有疑惑——我终于能正视自己的命运了。
t_en: But you helped remove any element of confusion — and I can finally face my destiny.

t_ch: 我生而愚笨…也因愚笨而死。我辜负了所有对我寄予厚望的人…我辜负了大守护者的职责和名誉。
t_en: I was born a fool... and died from my foolishness. I failed everyone who ever put their faith in me... I failed to uphold the duty and prestige of a Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 我失足踏入那敞开的箱门，以注定被世人耻笑的方式结束了自己的一生。
t_en: I slipped and fell into that open box, ending my life in a way that's destined for ridicule.

t_ch: 或许在生命终结的前一刻，我也曾被卷入羞愧的旋涡…但我已在这交界之域徘徊太久，早已感受不到悲伤或耻辱。我现在唯独渴望安宁。
t_en: Perhaps at the end of my life, I was caught up in a vortex of shame... but I've wandered in this purgatory far too long to feel any sadness or shame. All I want now is peace.

t_ch: 陌生人，我能感受到你的强大。与懦弱的我相比…你散发着如此坚强、镇定的气场。
t_en: Stranger, I can feel your power. Compared to my cowardice... you exude such a strong, calm aura.

t_ch: 我渴求你最后一次的施舍…希望你能赐予我最后的安宁。
t_en: I beg you for one last piece of generosity... I hope you can bless me with everlasting peace.

t_ch: …我明白了。
t_en: ...I understand what you mean.

t_ch: 谢谢你…谢谢你愿意留给我最后一丝尊严。
t_en: Thank you... thank you for leaving me with this one shred of dignity.

t_ch: …我下不了手。
t_en: I... I can't do it.

t_ch: …你要将我抛下吗？将我留在这无边无际的空虚中？
t_en: ...Are you going to leave me behind? Just leave me here in this endless void?

t_ch: 也罢…或许像我这样的人不配获得救赎。再见了，陌生人。
t_en: Well... maybe someone like me doesn't deserve to be saved. Farewell, stranger.

t_ch: …你回心转意了吗，陌生人？
t_en: ...Have you changed your mind, stranger?

t_ch: 我会赐予你安宁。
t_en: I will give you the peace you deserve.

t_ch: 谢谢你…谢谢你愿意留给我最后一丝尊严。
t_en: Thank you... thank you for leaving me with this one shred of dignity.

t_ch: 我果然还是下不了手…
t_en: I still can't bring myself to do it...

t_ch: …你要抛下我吗？将我留在这无边无际的空虚中？
t_en: ...Are you going to leave me behind? Just leave me here in this endless void?

t_ch: 也罢…或许像我这样的人不配获得救赎。再见了，陌生人。
t_en: Well... maybe someone like me doesn't deserve to be saved. Farewell, stranger.

t_ch: 谢谢你，陌生人……
t_en: Thank you, stranger...

t_ch: 对不起，斯捷潘先生…我不是…你要找的……
t_en: I'm sorry. Mr. Stefan... I'm not... who you're looking for...

t_ch: 希莉儿的残响被冰风的呼啸声溶解、吹散。
t_en: Cyrille's echoes dissipate amidst the howling of the icy wind.

t_ch: 一枚金色的饰品留在了地面上，安静地等待着你将它拾起。
t_en: A golden ornament remains on the ground, quietly waiting for you to pick it up.

t_ch: 捡起希莉儿的遗物。
t_en: Pick up Cyrille's relic.

t_ch: …我该碰它吗？
t_en: ...Should I even touch this?

t_ch: （这算什么问题？总得拿点东西当任务奖励吧。）
t_en: (It shouldn't be a big deal. It's only reasonable to take home some rewards for finishing all these missions.)

t_ch: （那个希莉儿，居然是曾经的大守护者…难以置信。）
t_en: (So Cyrille was actually a Supreme Guardian... how unbelievable.)

t_ch: （为什么她留下的裂界余响强烈到能聚集成人形？甚至还伴随着那些古怪的箱子谜题……）
t_en: (How can these echoes of hers be so powerful that they took on human form in the Fragmentum? And why was it accompanied by all those weird box puzzles...?)

t_ch: （带着她的遗物回上层区吧。肯定有人了解希莉儿担任大守护者的那段历史。）
t_en: (I should take her relic back to the Overworld. There must be someone who knows about Cyrille's past as a Supreme Guardian.)

t_ch: 你知道名叫希莉儿的大守护者吗？
t_en: Do you know the Supreme Guardian Cyrille?

t_ch: …呃，你说的是那位「愚者」希莉儿吧？
t_en: ...Mmm, do you mean Cyrille "the Fool"?

t_ch: 当然知道。换种说法，稍微学过点历史的人应该都知道吧？
t_en: Of course. I mean, anyone who's studied a little history has heard of her, right?

t_ch: 怎么突然想起问这个了？我可没空给你做知识科普啊…还有一大堆文件等着我盖章批复呢。
t_en: What made you suddenly interested in this piece of ancient history? I don't have the time for random trivia right now... There's a huge stack of documents on my desk waiting for my approval.

t_ch: 我找到了这个东西…
t_en: I found something...

t_ch: 嗯？什么……
t_en: Huh? What...

t_ch: 我从希莉儿那里拿到了这个。
t_en: I brought something back from Cyrille.

t_ch: …这是什么没品的玩笑吗？
t_en: ...Is this some kind of sick joke?

t_ch: 唉，算了…只看一眼倒也耽误不了几秒钟时间。你找到了什么玩意，拿给我看看吧。
t_en: Ah, okay... I guess, taking a look won't rob me of much time. What is it? Let me have a check, would you?

t_ch: 这…这是……
t_en: It's... this...

t_ch: 没想到啊，你可能真的找到了件不得了的古董……
t_en: I'm flabbergasted! You might've just found an incredible antique item...

t_ch: 根据《贝洛伯格前文明遗产保护条例》，通过民间活动获得的古玩古董需要上交给筑城者保管。你找到的这件饰物，我就先收下了。
t_en: Based on the Belobog Conventions for the Preservation of Ancient Relics, any antiques found by civilians must be turned over to the Architects for proper preservation. I will have to hold on to this ornament that you've found.

t_ch: 这是什么霸王条例？！
t_en: What? This is robbery in plain sight!

t_ch: 那我能拿到什么好处？
t_en: Then what's in it for me, huh?

t_ch: 别着急，当然不会让你空手离开。我很确定你找到的这件东西是真迹…一笔可观的奖金肯定是少不了的。
t_en: Relax, I won't leave you empty-handed. I'm highly confident that this thing is an authentic relic... I'm sure you'll be compensated handsomely.

t_ch: 哦，对了，你对贝洛伯格的历史了解不深吧？有关「愚者」希莉儿的历史资料，你可以在这本宣传册里找到。拿去吧，就当是政务部送你的纪念品。
t_en: Mmm, that's right — do you know much about Belobog's history? You can find out more about Cyrille "the Fool" in this pamphlet. Take it as a souvenir from the Administrative Department.

t_ch: （这里就是杨叔标记的调查区域了…四处看看吧。）
t_en: (This is the area that Mr. Yang had marked... Better take a look around.)

t_ch: 这是一座精美的冰雕——它与你在「黑塔」空间站中见到的虚卒是如此相似，令你不禁感叹寒潮的奇妙造化。
t_en: This is an exquisite ice sculpture. It resembles the Voidranger you saw in the Herta Space Station. It leaves you wondering at the amazing creations of the Eternal Freeze.

t_ch: （这里似乎没有类似信号源的装置……）
t_en: (Seems like there isn't any device here that looks like a signal source...)

t_ch: 封印着虚卒的冰块表面被皑皑白雪覆盖。尽管看不清被冰封之物的模样，你还是感受到了一股带有敌意的视线。
t_en: The surface of the ice that sealed the Voidranger is covered in snow. Despite not being able to see the icebound creature, you can still feel a hostile gaze.

t_ch: …等等，不对——它们真的有眼睛吗？
t_en: ...Wait. That's not right. Do they even really have eyes?

t_ch: （这里似乎没有类似信号源的装置……）
t_en: (Seems like there isn't any device here that looks like a signal source...)

t_ch: 如果雅利洛-Ⅵ的风雪确实持续了近七百年，这个可怜的虚卒恐怕也已在此处度过了相同的时间。
t_en: If the blizzard on Jarilo-VI has continued for almost <unbreak>700</unbreak> years, then this poor Voidranger has probably been stuck here for the same length of time.

t_ch: 如今，星核已经被封印，肆虐已久的寒潮想必也会慢慢散去。待此处气候恢复正常，它们从冰封中解脱时又会思考些什么？
t_en: Now that Stellaron has been sealed, this punishing Eternal Freeze probably will start to dissipate. When the climate here normalizes, what will these creatures think once they've been freed from the ice?

t_ch: …罢了，这个问题并不重要。毕竟你也不知道它们是否具备思考的能力。
t_en: ...Nevermind. That question doesn't matter. You don't know if they even have the ability to think.
(Huh? This is...)

t_ch: （原来不明信号只是「星际和平家电」的推销信息…把这件事告诉杨叔吧。）
t_en: (So, the unknown signal was actually an ad from the Interastral Peace Appliances... I'll let Mr. Yang know.)

t_ch: 转达不明信号的调查结果…
t_en: Report the findings of your investigation into the unknown signal...

t_ch: 你向瓦尔特与姬子说明了此行的调查结果……
t_en: You explain the results of the investigation to Welt and Himeko...

t_ch: 呵呵…有意思。这个结果还真是出乎意料呢。
t_en: Heh... Interesting. What a surprising turn of events.

t_ch: 虽然早知道公司在寒潮前就光顾了这颗行星，但据我所知，反物质军团正式登陆雅利洛-Ⅵ之前，公司的人就已经全部撤离了。
t_en: I knew the IPC had been on this planet before the Eternal Freeze, but as far as I was aware, it had pulled everyone out before the Antimatter Legion officially landed on Jarilo-VI.

t_ch: 没想到他们竟还派专人盯着被寒潮吞没的星球…可真像公司的办事风格。
t_en: I didn't think they would actually send someone to keep an eye on a planet engulfed in the Eternal Freeze... That however does sounds like their way of doing things.

t_ch: 既然只是推销信息，应该不会对雅利洛-Ⅵ形成威胁。有你确认我们就放心了。
t_en: Since it's just an ad message, it shouldn't pose a threat to Jarilo-VI. Thanks to your findings, we can all rest easy after all.

t_ch: 按照先前说好的，我和姬子已经为你准备了奖励……
t_en: As promised, Himeko and I have prepared a reward for you.

t_ch: …咦？你们谁的手机响了吗？
t_en: ...Huh? Whose phone is ringing?

t_ch: 寒潮过后的历史啊…我们的智库里似乎并没有相关文献。
t_en: History after the Eternal Freeze... Our data bank doesn't seem to have any relevant documents on this topic.

t_ch: 看来我们列车组的大忙人{NICKNAME}又接到新任务啦，加油喔。
t_en: Looks like the go-getter on our crew, {NICKNAME}, has another new mission. Good luck!

t_ch: 我该去哪里了解这段历史呢？
t_en: How can I learn about that history?

t_ch: 呵呵，这就是我们「开拓」的意义所在了。
t_en: Hehe. That's where our Trailblaze comes in.

t_ch: 真不想去啊…
t_en: I really don't want to go...

t_ch: 呵呵，就知道你会这么说。
t_en: Hehe. I knew you would say that.

t_ch: 在我看来，这倒是充实列车智库的好机会…想必你对这颗星球还谈不上非常了解，倒是不妨趁机了解一下相关历史——你觉得呢，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: The way I see it, this is a good opportunity to enrich the Express' data bank... I assume you don't yet know everything about this planet, and you might as well take this chance to learn a bit about its history. What say you, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 瓦尔特说得对。至于该去哪里了解这段历史嘛……
t_en: Welt is right. As for where you should go to learn about this history...

t_ch: 如果我没记错…在你们此次旅行里，应该与那位年轻的大守护者结下了缘分吧？
t_en: If my memory still serves me well... You've connected with a young Supreme Guardian during this journey. Isn't that right?

t_ch: 她应该是这颗星球上最接近历史真相之人。去向她请教一下，或许会有不少收获呢。
t_en: She is likely the person closest to the true history of the planet. Go and ask her. You might learn something.

t_ch: 那我去去就回。
t_en: I'll be on my way then.

t_ch: 要不先把奖励给我…？
t_en: How about giving me my rewards first?

t_ch: 呵呵，别心急嘛。本次的调查任务可是延长了哦，现在还不到领奖励的时候呢。
t_en: Hehe. Not so quick, {NICKNAME}. This investigation is now being extended, so it's not quite the time to dole out rewards just yet.

t_ch: …你就别欺负{NICKNAME}了。不过姬子说得不错——奖励就等这事彻底了结后再交给你吧。
t_en: ...Don't take advantage of {NICKNAME}. But Himeko does have a point. We will give you your reward after this matter has been completely resolved.

t_ch: 一路平安，{NICKNAME}。愿你此行能有所收获。
t_en: Have a safe trip, {NICKNAME}. I hope your journey will prove fruitful.

t_ch: 你对寒潮前的历史有了解吗？
t_en: Do you know about Belobog's history before the Eternal Freeze?

t_ch: 寒潮前的历史？我确实有所了解，但没办法很准确地为你复述出来。
t_en: Before the Eternal Freeze? I do know about it, but I can't recite it all by memory.

t_ch: 如果你感兴趣的话，不妨在旁边的书架上找找——那里有一套《贝洛伯格大事年表》，或许可以帮到你。
t_en: If you are really interested, you should look on the shelf over there for a set of books called Belobog Chronicles. They might be helpful references.

t_ch: 不过，你怎么突然想起要调查这段历史？是出什么事了吗？
t_en: What got you suddenly interested in researching that history anyway? Did something happen?

t_ch: 说来话长，我被某个人骚扰了…
t_en: Long story. It all started with an ad...

t_ch: 我需要补全列车组的智库档案。
t_en: I'm on a research mission for the Express' data bank.

t_ch: 原来如此……
t_en: I see...

t_ch: …唔，什么声音？
t_en: ...Oh. What was that sound?

t_ch: 噗…这下我完全明白了。
t_en: Pfft... I totally get it now.

t_ch: 那本《大事年表》就在书架上，你随便用吧。实在找不到的话就告诉我，我来帮你找。
t_en: That Chronicle is on the bookshelf. Feel free to have a look. If you can't find it, just let me know. I'll help you look.

t_ch: 不过，「星际和平家电」这个名字…总觉得有些耳熟。
t_en: The name Interastral Peace Appliances... does seem to ring a bell.

t_ch: 「星际和平家电」…「星际和平」……
t_en: Interastral Peace Appliances... Interastral Peace...

t_ch: 「星际和平公司」…这个名字，我好像在守护者的历史教材里读到过。似乎是个在星际间拥有巨大影响力的组织，还和「存护」有关……
t_en: Interastral Peace Corporation... I've read about it before in the Guardians' history textbook. I think it's some kind of interstellar organization that has massive influences all over the cosmos. It has something to do with Preservation...

t_ch: 唔…算了。虽然都是「琥珀王」的追随者，但应该暂时和我们贝洛伯格没关系。先不想了。
t_en: Oh... never mind. They're all followers of the Amber Lord, but they probably have nothing to do with Belobog. So I need not worry about them for now.

t_ch: 未必哦。
t_en: Not necessarily.

t_ch: 嗯…也是。不过就算将来取得联系，对贝洛伯格应该也是利大于弊……
t_en: Hmmm... maybe you're right. But I still believe they might be a net positive for Belobog if there comes a time when we do make contact with them in the future.

t_ch: 好了，你先去找需要的书吧。如果还需要帮助的话，记得随时告诉我哦。
t_en: Alright. Go and find the book you need. Just let me know if you need any help.

t_ch: 我先去找档案了，谢谢你。
t_en: I'll go look for the records then. Thank you.

t_ch: 别客气。希望克里珀堡的藏书能为你提供帮助。
t_en: You're welcome. I hope Qlipoth Fort's book collection will be of use to you.

t_ch: （《贝洛伯格大事年表》…想必就是这套书了。）
t_en: (Belobog Chronicles... It's probably this set.)

t_ch: （看看关于寒潮前后的部分吧……）
t_en: (Let me read about the years just before and after the Eternal Freeze...)

t_ch: （原来在寒潮来临前发生了这些事……）
t_en: (So that's what happened before the Eternal Freeze occurred...)

t_ch: （…咦？是手机响了吗？）
t_en: (...Huh? My phone's ringing?)

t_ch: （这人真要命啊…还是先回到列车组报告吧。）
t_en: (Ugh, these non-stop messages are driving me nuts... *sigh* Guess I should head back to the Express to report first.)

t_ch: 转达关于寒潮前历史的调查结果…
t_en: Report your findings of the history before the Eternal Freeze.

t_ch: 你向瓦尔特与姬子说明了关于寒潮前历史的调查结果……
t_en: You explain what you learned about the history prior to the Eternal Freeze to Welt and Himeko...

t_ch: 嗯…原来贝洛伯格是这么建成的呀。还有这套历法……
t_en: Hmmm... So that was how Belobog was established. Didn't know that they had a calendar system like this...

t_ch: 做得好，{NICKNAME}。这样一来，智库中关于寒潮前后的部分就可以填上了。
t_en: Good work, {NICKNAME}. And with that, we can finally have records on the time before and after the Eternal Freeze in our data bank.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}总是不负众望呢。看来，以后遇到什么事都可以放心交给你啦。那么，这次终于可以把奖励……
t_en: You never let us down, {NICKNAME}. I suppose if we ever have a problem, we can just leave it in your capable hands. Now then, we can finally give that reward to...

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}，你的手机似乎又响了。要看看吗？
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}. I think your phone is ringing again. Don't you want to check it?

t_ch: …真是个不屈不挠的人。这次调查任务可真是难为{NICKNAME}你了。
t_en: ...You're truly the very definition of indomitable. This investigation must've been quite the chore for you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 辛苦你了。来，这是我与姬子小姐为你准备的奖励。
t_en: Keep up the good work. Come, this is the reward that Himeko and I have prepared for you.

t_ch: 希望…这些能够弥补本次调查任务给你造成的精神伤害。
t_en: I hope... these can make up for any mental damage you've endured during this investigation.

t_ch: （刚才的推销员说「查宝」本该送往「黑塔」空间站……）
t_en: (Didn't the salesperson say that Findie was supposed to be taken to the Herta Space Station...)

t_ch: （去找艾丝妲问问好了……）
t_en: (I'll just go and ask Asta about it...)

t_ch: 这是你订购的家用寻物仪吗？
t_en: Is this the Home-Use Object Finder that you ordered?

t_ch: 咦？这是…和我当时在官网上看的预览图一模一样，就是有点脏。
t_en: Huh? This is... exactly like what I saw on the official website. Only this one is a bit dirty.

t_ch: 哦，不对。这款寻物仪的型号和我订购的不一样，好像是很久之前的老款……
t_en: Wait a minute. This finder is a different model from the one I ordered. It looks like an obsolete model from a long time ago...

t_ch: 你手上怎么会有这东西呀？发生什么事了？
t_en: How did you come by something like this? What happened?

t_ch: 你向艾丝妲说明了查宝的来历，以及那位锲而不舍的推销员的故事……
t_en: Telling Asta about Findie's origin and the story of a certain indefatigable salesperson...

t_ch: 乖、乖～不哭…「星际和平家电」的客服就是这样疯狂推销，根本不管客户的感受……
t_en: Now, now. Don't cry... Interastral Peace Appliances' customer service is all about crazy marketing. They don't care one bit how the customer feels...

t_ch: 下次再遇到这种事，你就告诉我，我让他们给你安排专属客服——咱不用受这种委屈！
t_en: Just let me know the next time you run into a problem like this. I'll have them arrange exclusive customer service for you. We don't have to suffer this kind of spamming ads!

t_ch: 不过，没想到这个小家伙居然被丢到了雅利洛-Ⅵ，还在那里度过了七百多年…真是一段神奇的故事。
t_en: Still, I never imagined this little one got sent out to Jarilo-VI and spent over <unbreak>700</unbreak> years there... What an incredible story.

t_ch: 谢谢你站长，站长最好了…
t_en: Thank you. You're the best...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，放心吧。钱能解决的事都不算事。
t_en: Hehe. Don't mention it. Any problem that can be solved with money is no real problem at all.

t_ch: 不过，我还是推荐你直接上「寰宇佳品」买东西，这网站的服务可是超一流的。
t_en: But, I still recommend that you buy things directly from Cosmo Premium. That site always has top-notch service.

t_ch: 所以它为什么会出现在那里？
t_en: So what was Findie doing there?

t_ch: 这个嘛——站长也感到很好奇。不过，我是说如果啊……
t_en: Hmmm. I find it strange, too. Then again, if...

t_ch: 如果，这个小家伙的经历和我那批消失的快递一样——那么根据「寰宇佳品」的说法就是：「因物流受到反物质军团等不可抗力的干扰」。
t_en: If this little one's experience was the same as my missing package, then according to Cosmo Premium, it was "because the shipment was impacted by an unavoidable interference, such as the Antimatter Legion."

t_ch: 当时他们通知我丢件之后，很快就发来了一封超级有诚意的道歉信，还给我安排了原路补发——比「星际和平家电」的服务可强太多啦。
t_en: After they notified me that my package went missing, they quickly sent out a super sincere apology letter and even arranged for a replacement. Their service is leaps and bounds ahead of "Interastral Peace Appliances."

t_ch: 你如果有什么需要就尽管告诉我哦，我可以帮你代购。只要记得回空间站取货就好啦。
t_en: If you ever need anything, just say the word. I can help you buy it. All you need to do is remember to come back to the space station to pick it up.

t_ch: 至于你带回来的这台「查宝」，就留给我作纪念吧～我可以把这个具有传奇经历的小家伙加入我的古董收藏。
t_en: As for the Findie that you brought back with you, you can leave it with me as a souvenir. I can add this little legendary gadget to my antique collection.

t_ch: 不过，如果你之后还需要用到它的话，随时和我说——它毕竟是你捡到的，随便你怎么用都可以哦。
t_en: If you need to use it in the future, you just let me know. After all, you were the one who picked it up. You can use it however you like.

t_ch: 哔嘟…这里不是「星际和平家电」门店。
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... This is not an Interastral Peace Appliances store.

t_ch: 我是谁？我在哪？我在做什么？
t_en: Who am I? Where am I? What am I doing?

t_ch: 原来这款寻物仪也会说话呀。
t_en: So this finder is able to talk.

t_ch: 「查宝」…对吧？你现在所在的位置是「黑塔」空间站哦，我之后会为你安排好住处的……
t_en: The name is "Findie"... right? Your current location is the Herta space station, and I'll later arrange accommodation for you...

t_ch: 哔嘟…我要回「星际和平家电」门店！我要回「星际和平家电」门店！
t_en: Beep beep... Must return to Interastral Peace Appliances! Must return to Interastral Peace Appliances!

t_ch: 我已经找到资料了，谢谢你。
t_en: I've found the what I needed, thanks.

t_ch: 嗯？已经找到了呀，太好了。希望它能帮上你的忙。
t_en: Hmm? Already found it? That's great. Hope it can help you.

t_ch: 如果还有什么需要就尽管和我说，克里珀堡一定会不遗余力地为你提供帮助。
t_en: Just let me know if you need anything else. Qlipoth Fort will pull out all the stops to offer you support.

t_ch: 我是受了司辰宫的委派…
t_en: The Palace of Astrum sent me...

t_ch: 你就是司辰宫派来体验「星槎测速员」的吧。
t_en: You're the person sent by the Palace of Astrum to experience being a starskiff speed tester, aren't you?

t_ch: 拿好，我们准备开始了。
t_en: Hold this. We're starting soon.

t_ch: 不需要先培训一下吗？
t_en: Don't I need any training?

t_ch: 你站在桥上，拿着计数器，看着桥洞下面，统计在规定的时间里有多少星槎通过。统计结束之后，然后把数字交给我。
t_en: Stand on the bridge and hold on to the counter, then look down and count how many starskiffs go past in a set time period. Once you're done, just tell me the count.

t_ch: 这也太简单了吧！
t_en: That's so easy!

t_ch: …你说只是站在桥上数数，对体验者来说缺乏挑战性了吗?
t_en: ...Are you saying that counting skiffs on the bridge is not challenging enough?

t_ch: 你明白我一直以来在干的都是什么样的工作了吧？
t_en: Now do you know the kind of work I've been doing all this time?

t_ch: 这工作真轻松啊…
t_en: What a relaxing job...

t_ch: 「你的工作真的有这么无聊吗？」「这就是传说中的薪水小偷吗？」
t_en: "Is your job really that boring?" "Is this what people mean by getting paid to do nothing?"

t_ch: 虽然没有说出口，但我听到你的心声了……
t_en: You didn't say it out loud, but I know what you're thinking...

t_ch: 这也太难了吧！
t_en: This is too hard!

t_ch: 乍听你这么说我还挺感动的。你，该不会是为了安慰我才这么说的吧？
t_en: Hearing you say that makes me feel a little better. You're not saying that just to console me, are you?

t_ch: 当然也不排除是你在说反话。
t_en: Of course, you might also just be sarcastic.

t_ch: 咳，「星槎测速员」这份工作，司辰宫里的同事们都觉得这是职场食物链的底端。谁要是被派来一日体验，都会觉得是对自己工作能力的羞辱。
t_en: Ahem. So, the position of starskiff speed tester is known by everyone in the Palace of Astrum as the bottom of the occupational pecking order. Anyone who got sent here for a one-day experience would think that this job is an insult to their intelligence and skills.

t_ch: 明明只要让机巧测速，然后开出罚单就行了，为什么还要有人来监督呢？
t_en: Why do we need someone to supervise when we could just deploy an ingenium to measure the speed and issue a ticket?

t_ch: 他们中大部分人都只是凭着第一印象对这差事做出粗暴的判断。统计当然可以由机器代劳，但是有了麻烦，还是得靠人亲自上阵才行啊。
t_en: Most people are just judging the job based on first impressions. Statistics can of course be done by a machine, but when there is trouble, it is still necessary to rely on humans like us.

t_ch: 这样吧，为了让你理解一下这份工作的麻烦程度，咱们不妨先来一场小小的测试吧。
t_en: How 'bout this, to give you an idea about how troublesome this job is, let's have a small quiz.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>星槎海的航道里会经过不同颜色、不同型号的星槎。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Starskiffs of different colors and models will pass through the lanes in Starskiff Haven.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>你需要在规定时间内，记下往来这里的所有星槎的型号和颜色。等到结束之后，我会向你提问。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>You will need to remember the models and colors of all starskiffs that pass by within the specified time period, and I will ask you a few questions at the end.</color>

t_ch: 比如我会问你<color=#dbc291ff>「蓝色的客运型星槎有多少艘？」</color>，这样一来，「测速」体验应该有些挑战性了，你看怎么样？
t_en: For example, I might ask <color=#dbc291ff>"How many blue passenger starskiffs went past?"</color> That should add some challenge to the experience. What do you think?

t_ch: 准备好了，咱们就开始。
t_en: We can start once you're ready.

t_ch: 开始吧！
t_en: Let's start!

t_ch: 等等，我还有些疑惑。
t_en: Wait, I have some questions.

t_ch: 规则是什么来着？
t_en: What are the rules again?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>星槎海的航道里会经过不同颜色、不同型号的星槎。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Starskiffs of different colors and models will pass through the lanes in Starskiff Haven.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>你需要在规定时间内，记下往来这里的所有星槎的型号和颜色。等到结束之后，我会向你提问。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>You will need to remember the models and colors of all starskiffs that pass by within the specified time period, and I will ask you a few questions at the end.</color>

t_ch: 比如我会问你<color=#dbc291ff>「蓝色的客运型星槎有多少艘？」</color>，需要根据你的观察，正确地回答我的问题。
t_en: For example, I might ask <color=#dbc291ff>"How many blue passenger starskiffs went past?"</color> You will need to answer my questions according to your observation.

t_ch: 星槎都有些什么型号？
t_en: What models are there?

t_ch: 星槎海街区目前只允许同行两个类型的星槎：<color=#dbc291ff>客运型和货运型</color>。
t_en: At the moment, there are only two types of starskiffs allowed on the streets of the Starskiff Haven: <color=#dbc291ff>passenger-type and cargo-type</color>.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>货运型的星槎</color>根据货运量区分，基本<color=#dbc291ff>只有红和绿两种颜色。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Freight starskiffs</color> are differentiated by the amount of cargo they carry, and are typically colored <color=#dbc291ff>either in red or green.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>客运型的星槎</color>根据虽然各不相同，基本<color=#dbc291ff>只有蓝、绿、白三种颜色。</color>
t_en: Although <color=#dbc291ff>passenger starskiffs</color> do vary, they generally come in <color=#dbc291ff>blue, green, or white.</color>

t_ch: 我没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 稍等，我还没准备好。
t_en: Wait, I'm not ready.

t_ch: 没事儿，等你准备好，我们再开始。
t_en: No worries. We can start once you're ready.

t_ch: 测速员体验…
t_en: Speed tester experience...

t_ch: 哦，准备好了？你要是准备好了，我们就开始吧。
t_en: Are you ready? We'll start whenever you are.

t_en: Let's start!

t_ch: 根据你的观察，以船身完整通过桥洞为标准，刚才<color=#dbc291ff>「白色的客运型星槎有多少艘？」</color>
t_en: According to your observation and counting only those that passed under the bridge entirely, <color=#dbc291ff>how many white passenger starskiffs went past?</color>

t_ch: 好像不太对。
t_en: That's not quite right.

t_ch: 没错，就是3艘。
t_en: That's right, the answer is 3.

t_ch: 好像不太对。
t_en: That's not quite right.

t_ch: 门前大桥下，游过一群槎。快来快来数一数，二四六七八。
t_en: 'Neath the bridge near the gate... starskiffs just passed straight. Come come, count them right... One two three four, eight in sight.

t_ch: 你发现，蓝色的客运型通过了3艘。
t_en: You saw three blue passenger starskiffs going past.

t_ch: 绿色的客运型通过了5艘。
t_en: You saw five green passenger starskiffs going past.

t_ch: 白色的客运型通过了6艘。
t_en: You saw six white passenger starskiffs passing through.

t_ch: 红色的货运型通过了1艘。
t_en: You saw one red passenger starskiff going past.

t_ch: 请问还要测试一次吗？
t_en: Would you like to test again?

t_ch: 我感觉还可以再来一次，多测试几次，我们才能知道效果如何嘛。
t_en: I think we can try again. The more we try, the better we can see how effective this is.

t_ch: 没问题，再来一次吧。
t_en: No problem. Let's do it again.

t_ch: …你说是就是吧。
t_en: ...Whatever you say.

t_ch: 就当是帮帮我嘛，再来一次。
t_en: Come on, help me one more time.

t_ch: 是星槎超速引起的事故！赶紧去瞧瞧有没有人受伤。
t_en: It's a traffic incident caused by a speeding starskiff! Let's go see if anyone's hurt.

t_ch: 门前大桥下，游过一群槎。快来快来数一数，二四六七八。
t_en: 'Neath the bridge near the gate... starskiffs just passed straight. Come come, count them right... One two three four, eight in sight.

t_ch: 一辆超快的星槎打断了你的思路，明显那是危险驾驶。
t_en: A speeding starskiff interrupts your thought process, obviously driving recklessly.

t_ch: 它穿过桥洞消失在视野里…还没等到你转身追上去，就只听到惨烈的车祸事故声音。
t_en: It disappears from your sight after zooming underneath the bridge... You hear a terrifying crash before you can even turn to give chase.

t_ch: 不好，我们快追上去！
t_en: Shoot, let's get there immediately!

t_ch: 幸好我们来得及时，我已经联系了丹鼎司医馆，急救小队一会儿就会赶过来。
t_en: Luckily we got here in time. I contacted the Alchemy Commission's apothecary and an emergency dispatch will be here soon.
